title: dryas integrifolia subsp. crenulata
intro: Dryas integrifolia subsp. crenulata, also known as the Arctic dryad or mountain avens, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the mountainous regions of the Northern Hemisphere. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dryas integrifolia subsp. crenulata are said to include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional remedies for skin conditions and digestive issues.
